title: Question about the Platinum Edition Holiday boxset
display_title: Question about the Platinum Edition Holiday boxset
wikitext:
[]: Question about the Platinum Edition Holiday boxset             Quote Post by Alastor » Fri Apr 01, 2011 2:40 pm Just need to ask this before I order it: it comes with four Director's Cut episodes, out of the six(?) which ones though?

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Fri Apr 01, 2011 2:48 pm Actually, only four episodes have director's cuts, and those are 21 through 24.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Apr 01, 2011 5:40 pm It comes with the only four Directors' Cut episodes there are: 21'-24'. The 25 and 26 "Director's Cuts" were actually just (partial? I don't remember) redubs of 25 and 26, but otherwise unchanged.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 01, 2011 5:44 pm gatotsu911 wrote: The 25 and 26 "Director's Cuts" were actually just (partial? I don't remember) redubs of 25 and 26, but otherwise unchanged.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Apr 01, 2011 5:47 pm Well, there you have it. ADV did partially redub the episodes as well, though, if I recall correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 01, 2011 6:10 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Well, there you have it. ADV did partially redub the episodes as well, though, if I recall correctly.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Apr 01, 2011 6:13 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: They redubbed the actual director's cuts. They redid a very few lines for Platinum.

[]:             Quote Post by RMZX » Fri Apr 01, 2011 10:23 pm What? The only John Swasey in the Platinum Edition was 21' thru 24'. The 25 and 26 are still MacAvery.

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Fri Apr 01, 2011 10:41 pm I'm also thinking about getting the Holiday Edition since it is the last release after all, and pretty damn cheap now. http://www.rightstuf.com/cgi-bin/catalogmgr/cci3tXBrBISYT6rueC/browse/item/81583/4/0/0 For those that may have it, is it a boxset or just a regular DVD case that encases all the episodes inside them with a thinpack or so?

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Fri Apr 01, 2011 10:47 pm It's a single-case "brick" package.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Fri Apr 01, 2011 10:55 pm Last two questions from me then: does it come with the thick stack of manuals that the individual releases did and is it a spindle styled one a la RahXephon: Complete Collection released around the same time?

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Fri Apr 01, 2011 11:12 pm svenge wrote: It's a single-case "brick" package.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sat Apr 02, 2011 5:40 am Alastor wrote: Last two questions from me then: does it come with the thick stack of manuals that the individual releases did and is it a spindle styled one a la RahXephon: Complete Collection released around the same time?

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Sat Apr 02, 2011 8:16 am Thanks. I have no problem with this one. I plan to get the Holiday edition then.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 02, 2011 1:15 pm Alastor wrote: Last two questions from me then: does it come with the thick stack of manuals that the individual releases did and is it a spindle styled one a la RahXephon: Complete Collection released around the same time?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Sat Apr 02, 2011 3:49 pm My copy still has that factory-fresh smell when I open it. Does that count as a perk of the packaging?

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Sun Apr 03, 2011 12:42 am gatotsu911 wrote: My copy still has that factory-fresh smell when I open it. Does that count as a perk of the packaging?

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Sun Apr 03, 2011 12:50 am So what exactly is the difference between the Holiday Edition and the standard Platinum Edition that I just bought last month? Aside from the packaging, they seem the same, price included.

[]:             Quote Post by Alastor » Sun Apr 03, 2011 12:54 am rhfxz1s wrote: So what exactly is the difference between the Holiday Edition and the standard Platinum Edition that I just bought last month? Aside from the packaging, they seem the same, price included.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Apr 03, 2011 8:46 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Really? I recall the Platinum releases of 25 and 26 being filled with John Swasey.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Fri Apr 01, 2011 2:48 pm Actually, only four episodes have director's cuts, and those are 21
